mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and raise equity discount rates, directly compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or the signal loses directional predictive power with p > 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, driving corporate earnings growth and positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output capacity and rising input costs, which compresses profit margins and leads to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles are followed by positive 20-day returns in >58% of observations, or the mechanism's predictive accuracy drops below random chance in tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising interest rates combined with slowing nonfarm payrolls growth creates stagflationary pressure that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, resulting in negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction between rising FEDFUNDS and declining PAYEMS yields a positive 20-day return in >60% of cases, or the joint signal shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP contraction below zero percent growth signals a recessionary onset, causing immediate risk-off positioning and negative short-term equity returns as investors price in earnings declines.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >50% of quarters where GDPC1 registers negative growth, or the signal fails to predict direction with statistical significance (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained inflation above 3% erodes real corporate earnings and prompts aggressive monetary tightening, generating negative equity returns over a three-month horizon as discount rates rise.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL levels above 3% are followed by positive 60-day returns in >65% of months, or the inflation-return correlation becomes positive and statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate alongside falling industrial production confirms a broad-based economic slowdown, reducing equity risk premiums and driving negative forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: The concurrent deterioration in UNRATE and INDPRO fails to predict negative 20-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from noise in out-of-sample validation.
